title: My Highest Recommendation Evangelion Decade
wikitext:
[]: My Highest Recommendation: Evangelion Decade             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 03, 2007 2:20 pm NGE Decade on Amazon I've been scarfing up NGE music lately and this was my latest purchase. Wasn't sure whether or not I'd dig the original J-poppy tunes, but it really surprised me. Not only did I enjoy it, but it's been one of my favorite CDs I've bought recently. I'm no expert on Japanese music, but I find it funny that they seem stuck in our 80s. Suffice it to say that, by and large, they do it better than we ever did. Track 3 is enough of a reason to recommend the album alone, but the rest of the album is of nearly equally high quality. I especially like the alternate version of Soul Refrain (I actually like the MUSIC on the new version better, but I think the melody sounds better on the disco-ish original version). I bought this at the same time as the 1 disc Refrain of Evangelion and I'm glad this CD includes the full version of Cruel Angel's Thesis and not the TV version. It also includes a Big Band (think Frank Sinatra) 10th Anniversary version of the song. The standbys are here as well; Fly Me to the Moon (original and Rei/Asuka/Misato mix) and Komm Susser Tod in the M-10 Edit version. Only complaint? No Everything You've Ever Dreamed. Granted, the disc is full, but I could've done without the extra Fly Me to the Moon version (always liked the original better than any of the remixes). If it was on here I wouldn't have to put Refrain into my player at the end just to play EYED at the end. All in all though it's a superb collection. It hasn't left my player for the last 2 weeks. Refrain is probably as good of a 1 disc retrospective as you'll find as well, so I highly recommend it too.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 03, 2007 2:39 pm Plus we also get the "new" song Tengoku no Kioku, Memories of Heaven sung by Rei (Hayashibara) to finish off the disc. Not that I have the actual disc, just the songs thanks to the Internets. My only complaint is they should have put Thanatos~if i can't be yours: 9 years after mix (I haven't heard it yet and I want to) instead of including that only on some "new" CD Single. "" denotes not really new anymore I guess. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 03, 2007 3:38 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: My only complaint is they should have put Thanatos~if i can't be yours: 9 years after mix (I haven't heard it yet and I want to) instead of including that only on some "new" CD Single.

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Tue Apr 03, 2007 5:09 pm There are so many Evangelion CDs out there, that whenever I see a new one on the shelves of a shop, I can't know for sure if I own every track in other releases. Sometimes they even label two different versions of a song with the same name. I may give a try to that "Decade" if I see it cheap. The last EVA CDs I bought were "The birth of Rei Ayanami" and the "Singles Collection", very interesting and with some unique versions.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 03, 2007 5:36 pm This is the CD Single that has the 9 year after mix of Thanatos: http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KICM-3122

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Apr 04, 2007 10:58 am What's special about M-10?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Apr 04, 2007 1:44 pm Kaysow wrote: What's special about M-10?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 04, 2007 4:32 pm Komm Susser Tod has the director's edit (what we hear in EoE) and the single version (people clapping and talking at the end). What S2 Works has besides this is "takes A and B, no intro" using the single version, though I have no idea what's the difference between these two. From what I understand, both Decade and Refrain of Evangelion CDs are Remastered*. *OST 1-3, DEATH and EoE were also remastered and re-released with DVD Audio, whatever that means.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Apr 05, 2007 7:58 am *Complete digression here, but for any interested, bear with me: There's several types of audio. CDA (CD audio) is the most popular. 'Higher Definition' versions of CDA (think LCD and DLP in TV terms) have come out in recent years to mild interest including SACD (Super Audio CD) and DVD-A (Obviously, DVD quality Audio). Of all of these, it's kinda up in the air which is actually "best". I guarantee my CD Player that just plays regular CDs but upsamples them - CDs play at 44.1kHz/16bit, my Reimyo upsamples to 176.4kHz/24bit - could destroy 98% of all SACD and DVD-A players out there. In all reality what's important isn't the format, but what's playing the format. CDs got such a bad rap over the years for sounding crappy and digital compared to LPs because CD players, until very recent years, have done a crappy job at turning those 0s and 1s into actual music. Now if you listen to a great CD player and LP player side by side you'd be hard pressed to tell which sounded more accurate to real music. Anyway, DVD-A played on an average DVD player will most likely sound better than the same CD playing the CDA on a CD player. This isn't because of the format, but because like I said: Most regular CD players do such a crappy job decoding CDA music, and DVD-A, on paper, is a higher resolution audio source. Also, not all remastering is of equally high quality. Remastering is an eyecatch title that's been thrown around a lot and few people actually know what it means. Many CDs that are 'remastered' are nothing more than 'repackaged'. Actual, careful remastering is a rare thing in the audio world, but every now and then I hear a CD that was supposedly 'remastered' done right (AC/DC's catalog got a superb remastering job).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 05, 2007 10:39 am I feel so "learn-ed" now! Seriously, I was wondering what was the deal with things recorded on DVD audio, eg could it play on regular CD players or what? Thanks for clearing that up!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Apr 05, 2007 10:46 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: I feel so "learn-ed" now! Seriously, I was wondering what was the deal with things recorded on DVD audio, eg could it play on regular CD players or what? Thanks for clearing that up!

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Thu Apr 05, 2007 11:37 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I think there's 3 different versions of Komm: The original ends with crowd noice (yelling, clapping, etc), the M10 doesn't have this. The M10 version also sounds much cleaner and clearer than the original (like it was remastered). Though if you're not into minute audio details you probably wouldn't notice it, it's not a major difference. But I'm an audiophile so I pay attention to those things.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 05, 2007 1:35 pm The clap is only in the ARM version of the OP, not the Yoko Takahashi version. (Part of the version I like the ARM version better ;))

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 05, 2007 1:45 pm I can't stand listening to the "Eva Girls" version of "Thesis"; it makes me cringe. Megumi Hayashibara's normal singing voice would be able to sustain that song*, but doing her Rei voice and sharing with Mitsuishi and Miyamura? Nuh-uh. Yoko Takahashi pwns the living daylights out of them. * I'm sure there is such a version around, but I neglected to pick it up.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 05, 2007 1:55 pm Yoko's versions is better, I must admit but in terms of personal preference I like Main II. btw, there are two(?) versions of Megumi doing Thesis. One that's a Rei version, the other just in her normal singing voice. Both can be heard via the Evamonkey Radio...thingy. ^^; In terms of Fly Me to the Moon, the original version by Clarie is my favorite, most certainly FTW* though the Eva Girls version is "cute". (Like I stated ages ago on here, with "Fry Me" it's like this: Hayashibara-most "professional" sounding, Mitsuishi-best pronunciation (She actually sings "worship" as opposed to "worsip" like all the other singers (minus Clarie and Yoko who say that right.) do), Miyamura-cutest sounding. ^^; )) *The versions of Fly Me that make ME cringe are Yoko's versions! :shudders: And although I think Ogata-sama's versions of Fry Me and Zanakoku are nifty (again, the Radio on this site has them, I think), it sounds like she has too much verbartto (sp??) in Fly Me and she rushes a bit in Zanakoku. Though I guess singing live can do that to you. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 06, 2007 8:51 am I think you were going for "vibrato" there. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 06, 2007 9:08 pm Yup, that's the word I was going for. ;) (I miss taking Chorus! I'm forgetting all the words I learned! T_T)

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Fri Apr 06, 2007 9:25 pm There are so many Cd's!!!! Which would you recomend?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 06, 2007 9:29 pm Opteron-O3 wrote: There are so many Cd's!!!! Which would you recomend?

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Fri Apr 06, 2007 9:48 pm Thanks :) *Fly me to the moon*
